lines: It would appear that we are...
Dr. A. Cula! Welcome. You arrive during, well... a moment.
Let me assure you first: the firm is not bankrupt. Bankruptcy is for firms whose paperwork has caught up with them. We are luckier than that, in a manner of speaking.
The rent is six weeks overdue.
Six weeks under the surface. The landlord knocks. Politely. The polite knock is somehow worse than a loud one.
It would appear that this can continue indefinitely, on momentum, which I am told is a recognised force in physics and almost recognised in accounting.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: There is also the printer lease, which has lapsed, and which I have been renewing by telephone every Thursday, and which the leasing company has stopped answering the telephone about, which is new.

lines: So. We carry on. Less every morning, but recognisably ourselves.
Anyway. I'm digressing. This is your first day, and we need you to start immediately. We have a case to take care of.
Mr. Murrow has the folder. Mr. Murrow knows the— ask Mr. Murrow.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: Right. Right. Yes. Welcome to Pig & Swine.

lines: Yes. Mr. Murrow. As I said. Apologies for the noise. Please don't forget to bill this conversation under "Business Development".
The procedural binder is in the archive, unless it has finally achieved sentience and crawled below deck.
The procedural binder. Good. Half the picture is better than none, though in court the other half tends to matter.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — S-grade. Voice draft from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Mr. Pig Perfect ('Can we sell this? No, wait, can we bill it?'), Taste-Standard pass adds the opening address per Pig's existing exclamatory cadence.
lines: Dr. A. Cula! Can we sell this? No — better — can we bill it?

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — D/F-grade. Voice draft from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Mr. Pig Bad ('That coffee smells like our accounts.'). Reads alongside the firm's existing six-weeks-underwater crisis joke.
lines: Dr. A. Cula. That coffee smells like our accounts.

states._comment: 2026-05-19 critique F9: previously-missing Beat 13 celebration speech that cula_b13_pig_celebration_response in cula.json was authored to react to. Firm-first / client-second ordering is load-bearing — the player should register that this firm celebrates its own survival before the client's. Maritime tic recedes (sincere undercut beat per style_canon §Mr. Pig arc rule; not the Ch5 fully-sincere moment, but the first hairline crack in the panic frame). once:true uses SAVE_VERSION 12 declarative fire-once (dialogue_states_seen) — no manual flag needed. Ordered before has_binder_and_memo so this state wins on Beat 13.
lines: Dr. A. Cula! We have survived another impossible thing.
The firm carries on. The client — yes, the client — also carries on. Both, mercifully, in that order.
I would offer a toast, but the only glasses in this office are the ones Mr. Murrow reads with.
The binder and the memo. I would say I am proud, but pride requires a moment of stillness and we do not currently have one.

idle_flavor: For the first time today, the ship is not actively sinking. It is merely making exploratory gurgling noises.
Has anyone seen my optimism? It was here this morning.
We cannot sail into court with a hole in the hull and optimism as our only oar.
